patch.value:
## Suggested domain

A small production facility is a useful stress test: sites contain lines; lines contain machines and sensors; assets depend on utilities and upstream processes; observations and maintenance commands change state; work orders and incidents connect operational context; safety and capacity rules are temporal invariants.

## Capabilities exercised

Custom item and event types; custom relationship registry; immutable command and observation history; derived snapshots and indexes; point-in-time reconstruction; topology and blast-radius graph queries; constraints and invariants; identity and authorship; schema migrations; import, export, merge, and federation; compact terminal output.

## Boundary

This is an exemplar and conformance test, not a production industrial-control system. It must reveal SDK gaps without moving domain-specific policy into core.
Beyond-PM SDK exemplar phase 2: temporal digital-twin graph with entity relationships, event replay, point-in-time state, and invariants
Prove the public pm SDK can model a non-PM digital twin whose entities, relationships, immutable events, snapshots, invariants, and queries are implemented without CLI-internal imports.

Choose and document one bounded domain such as a facility, production cell, fleet, supply network, or service topology with entities, typed relationships, states, commands, events, and invariants; Implement the exemplar entirely through published SDK exports and extension or package contracts; no CLI internal, core internal, or tracker-file access is allowed; Every mutation appends attributable immutable events; rebuilding from genesis and from verified checkpoints yields identical state and relationship indexes; Queries support current and point-in-time entity state, topology and impact traversal, event history, provenance, invariant violations, and bounded shell-friendly export; Model concurrent observations, conflicting updates, corrections by superseding events, schema evolution, offline or federated replicas, and deterministic merge or explicit conflict surfaces; Property and scenario tests cover replay, temporal relationships, invariant enforcement, partial failure, restore, import and export, and tamper evidence; Publish a structured gap report that links every missing capability to the existing SDK promotion lineage or a duplicate-checked new item
Universal project and context primitives through the public pm SDK
Validate that SDK item, field, relationship, event, query, history, and replay primitives can represent a realistic evolving physical or business system
Provides concrete proof that pm SDK abstractions generalize to digital twins and exposes missing primitives before they ossify
Broadens the SDK from project tracking to event-sourced domain engines, simulations, asset networks, organizations, and business logic
An installable exemplar package, realistic fixture, point-in-time queries, invariant evaluation, replay proof, and SDK gap report
The roadmap explicitly targets VCS and company-scale logic, but digital-twin capability has no canonical tracked exemplar

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Duplicate check 2026-07-13: exhaustive all-status search found the broad beyond-PM story pm-8ngt and VCS exemplar pm-xtrd but no digital-twin exemplar; this is phase 2 beneath that canonical lineage and depends on public graph primitives.

author: codex-root
message: Connect the temporal digital-twin exemplar to scale, conformance, policy, and evidence-gated portfolio governance

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Relationship rationale: the digital-twin exemplar must prove temporal replay and graph behavior under scale, conformance, and policy constraints, not merely demonstrate a domain schema. The roadmap-policy link keeps it exploratory until promotion evidence exists.

author: codex-root
message: Start broad digital-twin SDK exemplar implementation

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Implementation start 2026-07-23: building the bounded production-facility digital-twin exemplar as a first-party package using public @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk exports only. Scope covers temporal entity and relationship events, point-in-time replay, invariants, conflicts/corrections, schema evolution, federation/import-export, tests, docs, and packed-consumer proof. All-status duplicate check reused pm-kr3t beneath pm-8ngt; no new item created.

value.note: temporal replay graph invariant checkpoint and federation primitives
100 percent package coverage plus installed CLI acceptance
capability gap inventory and canonical lineage
runnable facility workflow and SDK boundary

patch.value: Digital-twin package consumes host-injected graph primitives without runtime package resolution

value.note: 100 percent package coverage plus installed CLI acceptance

patch.value: Bundled catalog and all-alias installation coverage

value.note: 100 percent package coverage plus installed CLI acceptance

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Evidence: a freshly rebuilt global pm binary initialized an isolated temporary workspace, installed digital-twin, created facility/machine/utility entities with normalized pm-* IDs, added a utility dependency, reconstructed machine state at event time, surfaced utility_not_running, idempotently re-imported four events as skips, and verified a valid checkpoint with a 3-node/1-edge graph. The temporary workspace was moved to trash after verification.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Evidence correction: the temporary filesystem does not support trash. After the successful proof, the exact isolated path /tmp/tmp.yG3Q12ZmSw was verified and deleted directly; it contained only this run's disposable tracker and global extension state.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: The installed-package failure found during full-suite TDD was architectural: value imports from @unbrained/pm-cli/sdk cannot resolve after a bundled extension is copied into an otherwise empty tracker. The durable fix was to expand host-injected ExtensionCommandSdk services and keep package runtime SDK imports type-only.

patch.value: Delivered the public-SDK-only temporal digital-twin package with custom schema, immutable events, point-in-time replay, topology/invariants, impact, replica merge, checkpoints, import/export, installed CLI proof, docs, and 100% coverage.
Shipped @unbrained/pm-digital-twin as a first-party, public-SDK-only exemplar backed by host-injected graph/event primitives and no runtime private imports.

A clean consumer can install a digital-twin package, create custom facility entities, append and replay temporal state/topology, evaluate invariants and impact, reconcile offline streams, and export/import verified checkpoints through public SDK contracts.

The package provides seven domain commands, two custom item types, five fields, four relationship kinds, event-time replay, corrections/conflicts/schema evolution, graph invariants and impact, offline merge, checkpointed bounded bundles, transactional creation/compensation, complete docs, clean installed CLI proof, and 100% coverage.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: PR evidence: GitHub PR #656 (https://github.com/unbraind/pm-cli/pull/656) carries the temporal digital-twin package, host-injected SDK graph primitives, timestamp replay correction, documentation, and strict coverage/installed-consumer evidence at reviewed head 898fc04764cbc99d2db361ccdbee47554c10bb8d. First hosted pass: every repository-owned check green; CodeRabbit produced no actionable thread within the review window; Codecov confirmed all modified coverable lines tested; external Gemini/Sourcery/Greptile review availability is limited by sunset or quota and is not a code failure.
